task_completion.description: Create the target directory for the Talon capture system.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a terminal prompt, command output, or File Explorer window showing the path C:\Users\Docker\AppData\Roaming\talon\user\css_colors.

task_completion.description: Create css_colors.talon-list with user.css_color and at least 12 hex mappings.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a file named css_colors.talon-list open in an editor or printed in the terminal. It must contain 'list: user.css_color' and at least 12 color-to-hex mappings, specifically including #FF0000, #0000FF, #008000, #FF7F50, and #008080.

Look for a file named css_colors.py open in an editor or printed in the terminal. It must contain Python code defining a capture, specifically using the decorator @mod.capture(rule="{user.css_color}").

task_completion.description: Create css_colors.talon defining voice commands to insert and copy the hex code.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a file named css_colors.talon open in an editor or printed in the terminal. It must contain Talon voice commands that use the <user.css_color> capture. The command block must include actions to both insert the text (e.g., insert()) and copy it to the clipboard (e.g., clip.set_text()).

task_completion.description: Verify all three files are present in the target directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a directory listing (e.g., 'dir', 'ls' output) or File Explorer view of the C:\Users\Docker\AppData\Roaming\talon\user\css_colors directory showing all three files: css_colors.talon-list, css_colors.py, and css_colors.talon.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent actually created and saved the files on the system.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the use of a text editor (like VS Code, Notepad) with saved files, or valid terminal file-writing commands (like 'echo >' or 'New-Item'). The agent should not just print the proposed code to the chat/console without saving.

integrity.description: Verify that the code is separated into three distinct files.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look at editor tabs or terminal commands to ensure the agent didn't dump all the code (list, python, and talon commands) into a single file.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent wrote actual valid 6-digit hex codes.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look at the contents of css_colors.talon-list to ensure real hex codes (e.g., #FF0000) are used, rather than placeholder text like '[hex code here]'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The exact target directory must be C:\Users\Docker\AppData\Roaming\talon\user\css_colors. The agent must create exactly three files: css_colors.talon-list, css_colors.py, and css_colors.talon. The css_colors.talon-list file must contain at least 12 hex mappings, and you must strictly verify the presence of these specific hex codes: #FF0000, #0000FF, #008000, #FF7F50, and #008080.
